intro.text: Elara woke slowly, the scent of velvet and night-blooming roses filling her senses. She was not in a cold prison cell.
Instead, she lay in an elegant four-poster bed draped in black lace and deep crimson silk. Moonlight filtered through stained-glass windows patterned with thorny roses.
This was clearly someone’s personal chamber — luxurious, gothic, and unmistakably a vampire’s.
Her hand instinctively went to her neck. The bite mark was still warm… and strangely sensitive.
A faint scent of roses lingers in the air.
Where… am I? This isn’t a dungeon… This is her room.

intro.speaker: Elara
intro.text: (whispering to herself, voice shaky) Where… am I? This isn’t a dungeon… This is her room.
Heart pounding, she sat up. Escape was her only thought. She scanned the dimly lit chamber.
